meta.title: Hydration and Route Guards
meta.excerpt: Preventing hydration mismatches and implementing secure route protection patterns.

seo.metaTitle: Hydration and Route Guards | App Reference
seo.metaDescription: Avoid hydration issues and secure your routes with proper guards.

blocks.content: Hydration mismatches occur when server-rendered HTML differs from client-rendered output. Route guards protect sensitive pages.

blocks.title: Protection Layers
blocks.content: Implement guards at multiple layers: middleware for routes, server components for data, and client components for UI.
Use these patterns to avoid hydration mismatches while maintaining optimal user experience.

blocks.title: Safe Client-Only Code Pattern
Hydration-Safe Approach
blocks.content: Always render a placeholder on the server that matches the initial client render, then update after mounting.
Next.js middleware provides edge-level route protection, running before page rendering.

blocks.title: Middleware Route Guard Example
Edge Runtime Limitations
blocks.content: Middleware runs on edge runtime with limited Node.js APIs. Use server components for complex authentication logic.
